question.label: Settling in
question.name: How has week three felt from your side, Sam?
question.description: Safe opener that lets Sam set context before anything specific is raised.

answer: I can do the tasks, but I still second-guess whether my approach matches team expectations.
note: He can do the tasks but is second-guessing whether his approach matches team expectations, suggesting mild drag from uncertainty while still showing basic engagement; also reveals a strong off-signature clarity concern.

question.label: Team expectations
question.name: When you second-guess your approach, what expectations feel hardest to read?
question.description: Drills into the specific expectation gap he just named so the manager can remove ambiguity quickly.

answer: Local setup is better now, but I still lose time when the design and API details do not match.
note: He names a specific expectation gap: mismatched design and API details are creating rework and slowing him down.

question.label: Design/API mismatch
question.name: When design and API details do not match, where does it usually break first?
question.description: Pins down the exact mismatch point so the manager can remove a concrete onboarding ambiguity.

answer: I am not always sure who should approve what before I push.
note: He names a concrete approval-path ambiguity before shipping, which is a strong clarity gap in onboarding.

question.label: Handoff gaps
question.name: Where are the handoffs still fuzzy for you?
question.description: Finds the exact transition points where Sam is compensating or guessing.

answer: I usually wait too long before asking because I want to solve things myself first.
note: He is compensating for fuzzy handoffs by delaying questions, which signals a strong clarity gap around when and who to ask.

question.label: Wait too long
question.name: When you hold back on asking, what are you usually unsure about specifically?
question.description: Drills into the exact uncertainty behind his delayed asks so the manager can remove that friction.

answer: When feedback is specific and fast, I move much quicker.
note: Names a missing condition — specific, fast feedback — as what unlocks his pace, which signals both unclear standards and insufficient growth support; [BUDGET-STARVED]

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: feedback specific fast, — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I still feel fuzzy on where quality bars differ between quick fixes and core flows.
note: He names a specific clarity gap in shipping standards — the quality bar differs by work type and still feels fuzzy; [THREAD-DEFERRED] quick fixes vs core flows quality bar.

question.label: Go-to people
question.name: Who have you found easiest to go to when you get stuck?
question.description: Surfaces which relationships are already working and where support can be routed.

answer: Pairing once or twice a week would probably prevent me from getting stuck silently.
note: Deficiency-as-request: he names missing pairing as the support that would stop silent stalls, signalling mild engagement drag and clarity gap.

question.label: Next support
question.name: What support from me would help you move faster this fortnight?
question.description: Closes on a concrete manager action for the next stretch of onboarding.

answer: If we define what good looks like for my next two tickets, I will feel much steadier.
note: Names a specific missing support need — defining what good looks like on the next two tickets — which signals current clarity and ramp support are still insufficient.

question.label: Priority ranking
question.name: If you had to cut your work in half tomorrow, what would you drop first — and what's non-negotiable?
question.description: Forces a real priority ranking. Vague answers are a clarity signal.

answer: I want to start by pairing on my next ticket so I can see the approval bar in practice.
note: He names pairing on the next ticket to see the approval bar in practice, which signals a strong current clarity gap and some proactive engagement.
